part.text:
<system-reminder># System Information

Operating System: linux
Default Shell: 
Home Directory: /root
Current Working Directory: /
Current Time: Tue Jun 09 2026 06:11:40 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time)

# GIT STATUS
Current branch: 
Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): 

Recent commits:


# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

# RedwoodSDK: Nested Layouts ## Background A RedwoodSDK project is pre-installed at `/home/user/myapp`. Use the `layout()` helper from `rwsdk/router` to wrap a group of pages with a shared `AppLayout`, and then nest an `AdminLayout` for `/admin/*` pages. ## Requirements * Create `src/app/layouts/AppLayout.tsx` exporting a server component `AppLayout` that wraps `children` with HTML containing a `
` element with the text `RedwoodSDK Demo` and a `
` element with the text `© rwsdk demo`. * Create `src/app/layouts/AdminLayout.tsx` exporting a server component `AdminLayout` that wraps `children` with HTML containing an `
` element with the text `Admin Sidebar`. * In `src/worker.tsx`, compose layouts: every page must be wrapped in `AppLayout`; pages under `/admin/*` must additionally be wrapped in `AdminLayout`. ## Acceptance Criteria * Project path: /home/user/myapp * Start command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5173 * Port: 5173 * Routes (must use `render(Document, layout(AppLayout, [...]))` style): * `GET /` → HTML contains `RedwoodSDK Demo` (from AppLayout header), `© rwsdk demo` (from AppLayout footer), and `

Public Home

`. HTML must NOT contain `Admin Sidebar`. * `GET /about` → HTML contains AppLayout markers and `

About

`. HTML must NOT contain `Admin Sidebar`. * `GET /admin/dashboard` → HTML contains AppLayout markers, the `Admin Sidebar` aside, and `

Admin Dashboard

`. * `GET /admin/users` → HTML contains all three of: AppLayout header text, `Admin Sidebar`, and `

Admin Users

`.
I'll start by exploring the project structure to understand the existing codebase.
Let me focus on the relevant source files:

output.content:
{
  "name": "@redwoodjs/starter",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A bare-bones RedwoodSDK starter",
  "main": "index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "keywords": [],
  "author": "",
  "license": "MIT",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "dev": "vite dev",
    "dev:init": "rw-scripts dev-init",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "worker:run": "rw-scripts worker-run",
    "clean": "npm run clean:vite",
    "clean:vite": "rm -rf ./node_modules/.vite",
    "release": "rw-scripts ensure-deploy-env && npm run clean && npm run build && wrangler deploy",
    "generate": "rw-scripts ensure-env && wrangler types --include-runtime false",
    "check": "npm run generate && npm run types",
    "types": "tsc"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "19.2.6",
    "react-dom": "19.2.6",
    "react-server-dom-webpack": "19.2.6",
    "rwsdk": "1.2.11"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cloudflare/vite-plugin": "1.33.2",
    "@cloudflare/workers-types": "4.20260426.1",
    "@types/node": "~25.6.0",
    "@types/react": "19.2.14",
    "@types/react-dom": "19.2.3",
    "typescript": "6.0.3",
    "vite": "~7.3.2",
    "wrangler": "4.85.0"
  },
  "pnpm": {
    "onlyBuiltDependencies": [
      "esbuild",
      "sharp",
      "workerd"
    ]
  }
}

export const Document: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({
  children,
}) => (
  <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <meta charSet="utf-8" />
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
      <title>@redwoodjs/starter-minimal</title>
      <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
      <link
        rel="preconnect"
        href="https://fonts.gstatic.com"
        crossOrigin=""
      />
      <link
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Sans:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400..900;1,400..900&display=optional"
        precedence="first"
      />
      <link rel="modulepreload" href="/src/client.tsx" />
    </head>
    <body>
      {children}
      <script>import("/src/client.tsx")</script>
    </body>
  </html>
);

part.text: Now let me check the rwsdk router API to understand `layout()`:
Now let me check the types for LayoutProps:
Now I have a clear understanding of the API. Let me create the todo list and start implementing.
Now let me create the page components. I need to update the Home page and create About, Admin Dashboard, and Admin Users pages:

Now let me update `worker.tsx` with the nested layout composition. The pattern is: `render(Document, layout(AppLayout, [...routes...]))`. For admin routes, I need to nest `AdminLayout` inside `AppLayout`. The way to do this with the `layout()` API is to compose them:
Now let me start the dev server and test the routes:

Public Home

`, `© rwsdk demo` - **GET `/admin/users`**: ✅ `RedwoodSDK Demo`, `Admin Sidebar`, `

`, `© rwsdk demo`

input.result:
Implemented nested layouts in the RedwoodSDK project. Here's a summary of all changes: ### Files Created **`src/app/layouts/AppLayout.tsx`** — Server component wrapping `children` with a `
RedwoodSDK Demo
` and `
© rwsdk demo
`. **`src/app/layouts/AdminLayout.tsx`** — Server component wrapping `children` with an `
Admin Sidebar
`. **`src/app/pages/about.tsx`** — Page component rendering `

About

`. **`src/app/pages/admin/dashboard.tsx`** — Page component rendering `

Admin Dashboard

`. **`src/app/pages/admin/users.tsx`** — Page component rendering `

Admin Users

`. ### Files Modified **`src/worker.tsx`** — Uses the `layout()` helper from `rwsdk/router` to compose nested layouts: ```tsx render(Document, layout(AppLayout, [ route("/", Home), route("/about", About), layout(AdminLayout, [ route("/admin/dashboard", AdminDashboard), route("/admin/users", AdminUsers), ]), ])) ``` **`src/app/pages/home.tsx`** — Simplified to render `

Public Home

`. ### Route Verification (all passing) | Route | AppLayout header | AppLayout footer | Admin Sidebar | Page content | |---|---|---|---|---| | `GET /` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ (correctly absent) | `

Public Home

` | | `GET /about` | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ (correctly absent) | `

About

` | | `GET /admin/dashboard` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | `

Admin Dashboard

` | | `GET /admin/users` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | `

Admin Users

` |
